skill.name: sds-msds-risk-scanner
skill.description: Extract hazard codes and safety info from chemical safety datasheets.
skill.summary: Extract GHS hazard codes and precautionary statements from chemical safety datasheets.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 73 static analysis findings are false positives. GHS hazard code patterns (H-codes like H225, P-codes like P280) were misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithms by the static scanner. Markdown backtick code blocks in SKILL.md and references files were misidentified as Ruby shell backtick execution. Security checklist items prohibiting path traversal (../) were misidentified as actual path traversal. The skill is a legitimate chemical safety data scanner with documented Python script execution and filesystem access for safety analysis. No prompt injection or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: GHS hazard code patterns misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: H-code and P-code regex patterns (H\d{3}[dfi]?, P\d{3}[abc]?) are GHS classification standards for chemical hazards, not cryptographic algorithms. These are standard safety classification codes used globally in chemical safety datasheets.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks misidentified as shell backtick execution
low_findings.description: Backtick characters in SKILL.md and references files are standard Markdown code formatting, not Ruby shell execution. No actual command injection or shell execution via backticks exists in the skill.

low_findings.title: Security checklist items misidentified as path traversal
low_findings.description: SKILL.md lines 115 and 118 contain security checklist items that explicitly PROHIBIT path traversal (../). These are security controls documenting safe practices, not actual path traversal attacks.

low_findings.title: GitHub source URL flagged as network risk
low_findings.description: The GitHub repository URL in SKILL.md is the legitimate source reference for the skill, not a malicious network endpoint. No actual network calls are made by the skill during operation.

content.user_title: Scan chemical safety datasheets for hazards and risk levels
content.value_statement: Manually reading Safety Data Sheets (SDS/MSDS) for chemical hazard information is slow and error-prone. This skill extracts GHS hazard codes, precautionary statements, and risk levels from chemical safety documents automatically.
actual_capabilities: Extract GHS H-codes (hazard statements) from SDS text with descriptions
Extract P-codes (precautionary statements) from chemical safety documents
Assess overall risk level based on detected hazard code combinations
Return structured hazard code descriptions with safety classifications
Support demo mode for quick testing and validation
Identify fatal, corrosive, flammable, and irritant hazard classifications
limitations: Requires pre-digitized SDS text as input (no built-in PDF parsing)
Covers a limited set of GHS hazard classes (9 predefined codes in dictionary)
Does not support multi-language or non-GHS classification systems
Risk assessment is based on code presence only, not concentration or exposure data

use_cases.title: Lab Safety Training Support
use_cases.description: Extract hazard information from SDS documents to create training materials for laboratory personnel.

use_cases.title: Compliance Documentation
use_cases.description: Generate structured hazard summaries from chemical safety datasheets for regulatory compliance reporting.

use_cases.title: Emergency Response Planning
use_cases.description: Quickly scan SDS documents to identify critical hazards and PPE requirements during emergency planning.
use_cases.target_user: Emergency response coordinators and safety inspectors

prompt_templates.title: Basic Hazard Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt:
Extract all GHS hazard codes from this SDS document and explain what each one means:

[SDS text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick lookup of hazard codes from a single chemical safety datasheet

prompt_templates.title: Full Safety Scan
prompt_templates.prompt:
Scan this SDS for both H-codes and P-codes. Then assess the overall risk level and provide PPE recommendations:

[SDS text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete safety analysis with risk assessment and protective measures

prompt_templates.title: Batch Chemical Review
prompt_templates.prompt: I have safety datasheets for the following chemicals: [list chemicals]. For each one, identify the GHS hazard codes, determine the risk level, and rank them from most to least hazardous.
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-chemical hazard comparison for lab inventory management

prompt_templates.title: Documented Safety Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt:
Run the complete safety scanning workflow for this SDS document. Include assumptions, risk levels, precautionary statements, and any missing information that would affect the final safety assessment:

[SDS text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Full audit-ready safety analysis with documented methodology

output_examples.input: Scan this SDS for acetone hazards: Product: Acetone. Hazard Statements: H225, H319, H336. Precautionary Statements: P210, P233.
output: Risk Level: HIGH - Use full PPE required

Hazard Codes:
  H225: Flammable liquid and vapor
  H319: Causes serious eye irritation
  H336: May cause respiratory irritation

output_examples.input: Identify safety concerns for Chemical X. SDS data shows: Hazard Statements: H314, H318, H226.
output: Risk Level: EXTREME - Handle with extreme caution

Hazard Codes:
  H314: Causes severe skin burns and eye damage
  H318: Causes serious eye damage
  H226: Flammable liquid and vapor
PPE Recommendation: Full protective equipment required

best_practices: Always verify extracted hazard codes against the original SDS document before use
Document the SDS source date and version to ensure current hazard information
Confirm the GHS classification system version matches the region of use
anti_patterns: Using the scanner to assess chemical mixtures without individual component SDS data
Relying solely on automated extraction without human validation of critical hazards
Applying the built-in risk assessment beyond its documented scope of 9 GHS hazard classes

faq.question: What types of hazard codes does this skill extract?
faq.answer: It extracts GHS H-codes (hazard statements) and P-codes (precautionary statements) from chemical safety datasheets.

faq.question: Does this skill replace a formal chemical safety review?
faq.answer: No. This is an automated extraction tool. Always verify results against the original SDS and consult a certified safety professional.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with PDF safety datasheets?
faq.answer: The skill works with digitized text input. You may need to convert PDF to plain text before using the scanner.

faq.question: What hazard classes are included in the code dictionary?
faq.answer: The dictionary covers common GHS classes: fatal toxicity, corrosive damage, flammability, skin and eye irritation, and respiratory irritation.

faq.question: How does the automated risk assessment work?
faq.answer: It classifies risk as EXTREME (fatal codes present), HIGH (corrosive codes), MODERATE (3 or more hazard codes), or LOW (basic safety measures).

faq.question: Can I add custom hazard codes to the scanner?
faq.answer: The current version uses a predefined code dictionary in the Python script. Custom codes would require modifying the source code.
